laws.title: Public Law 11-73 | Let Me Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repealed acting-service enactment; no current operative text remains.
laws.chronology: The recovered source is a general acting-service rule, but the authoritative mass report classifies the law as repealed/nonoperative, so it remains history only.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No surviving transfer or redesignation issue remains because the law is treated as repealed history only.
laws.status_basis: The mass report classifies the law as repealed based on the Trello record, and no current Code operation is authorized.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked in Title 22 even though the law is repealed history only; a concise historical note is sufficient and no operative codification is needed.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise repeal-history note.
Do not codify operative text or a new Title 5 section.

laws.title: Public Law 12-74 | Federal Bar Association Act of 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: New executive-branch Federal Bar Association statute establishing the Association and its administrative functions.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later superseding repeal was found in the reviewed materials. The repeal in section 3 is a historical repeal of prior Public Law 7-47, not a repeal of this act.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 3 is a repeal of a prior public law only; sections 4 and 5 create new Title 5 organic provisions and do not transfer existing Code text.
laws.status_basis: Sections 4-5 establish a generally applicable permanent executive-branch entity, and no later repeal of the act was identified.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML treatment is a boilerplate note at a Title 50 anchor, but the act needs real Title 5 codification for the new association and its governing provisions.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note.
Codify sections 4 and 5 as new Title 5 organic provisions.
Record the repeal of Public Law 7-47 as historical repeal context only.

laws.title: Public Law 12-75 | Freedom of Information Common Sense Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Direct amendment to 5 U.S.C. section 552 shortening FOIA response and appeal deadlines to four days.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later superseding repeal was found in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the act directly replaces text in 5 U.S.C. section 552.
laws.status_basis: Section 3 expressly replaces text in 5 U.S.C. section 552, so the act is a live Code amendment.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is parked in Title 44, but the real target is 5 U.S.C. section 552 and the replacement text still needs to be inserted.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note.
Amend 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(6)(A)(i), (ii), and (ii)(I) with the source language.
Retain a drafting-cleanup flag for the residual 20-day-period wording.

laws.chronology: Source-limited. The public-law record is unrecoverable, so no lawful later-law or repeal chronology can be fixed from the source itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain could be resolved from the available source.

laws.title: Public Law 12-80 | Government Labor Management Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical labor-management enactment; later repealed in full before codification.
laws.chronology: The mass report identifies the act as superseded before codification, and later enactment PL-031-226 repeals Public Law 12-80 in its entirety.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal by PL-031-226 leaves no surviving transfer or redesignation requiring operative text.
laws.status_basis: PL-031-226 repeals Public Law 12-80 in its entirety, so no current Code insertion is authorized.

current_implementation.detail: The retained note is parked in Title 29 and still carries a source dump, but the law is repealed history only and should not be codified as operative labor law.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise repeal-history note.
Do not codify any Title 5 labor-management chapter for this repealed act.

laws.title: Public Law 12-81 | The Integrity Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Statutory-note enactment at 2 U.S.C. section 6567 governing Senate consultation before certain treaty or organization withdrawals.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repeal was found in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the rule is treated as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. section 6567.
laws.status_basis: The act is a general and permanent enactment, but the canonical report treats it as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. section 6567.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is in Title 22, but the canonical anchor is 2 U.S.C. section 6567; the note form is legally sufficient once moved to the correct anchor.

recommended_actions: Move the note to 2 U.S.C. section 6567.
Keep the treatment as a statutory note, not operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 12-82 | Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act 2023
laws.final_statutory_text: Direct-amendment military-officer commissioning reform; 10 U.S.C. section 531, section 12203, and section 151 are amended and section 12204 is repealed.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repealer was found in the reviewed materials. The current XML reflects only the section 151 change, so the other section amendments still need codification.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 5(a) is a repeal of an existing Code section, while sections 4, 5(b), and 9(a) are direct amendments to existing sections. No redesignation issue remains.
laws.status_basis: Sections 4, 5(a), 5(b), and 9(a) directly amend existing Title 10 provisions, and the law remains operative.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML only reflects the section 151 change; the amendments to sections 531, 12203, and 12204 still need to be reflected in the code set.

recommended_actions: Codify the section 531, 12203, and 151 amendments and the section 12204 repeal.
Keep the existing section 151 note as amendment history, but do not treat it as the whole act.
